purpose: Support transferable MOE pharmacophore workflow iteration by separating literature methods, validation evidence, and generalizable mechanism rules.
important_boundary: Do not encode target names, PDB IDs, residue numbers, feature labels, or article-specific coordinates into generic rules.
Use article evidence to extract physical-chemical principles: feature perspective, pocket segmentation, dominant chemical family, redundancy compression, and validation logic.
Ligand-based training-set models and structure-based MOE Query models must not be mixed as if they had the same ground truth.

method_classes.ligand_based_training_set: HypoGen/3D-QSAR or active/inactive training-set pharmacophore. Useful for validation strategy, GH/enrichment thinking, and feature count discipline; not a direct structure-reproduction target.
method_classes.structure_based_moe_query: Complex- or pose-derived MOE query pharmacophore. Useful for testing feature perspective, coordinates, family compression, and selected-feature curation.
method_classes.hybrid_virtual_screening: Pharmacophore plus docking, MD, MM/GBSA, SAR, or biological assay filtering. Useful for multi-stage prioritization and deciding which pharmacophore points should survive compression.
method_classes.peptide_interface_design: Protein-peptide or peptide-like interface model. Useful for segment-aware anchors, backbone-register constraints, and distinguishing side-chain hotspots from generic polar contacts.

entries.pharmacophore_source: active/inactive ligand training set and validation set
what_it_can_teach: Use training-set diversity and decoy/enrichment validation to judge whether a compact model is selective.
A model can be scientifically useful even when it is not structure-derived; do not force it into PDB-coordinate comparison.
Feature count should be disciplined by predictive ability, not by how many contacts are chemically imaginable.
what_not_to_copy: Do not treat ligand-based feature positions as receptor-pocket ground truth unless the coordinate frame has been explicitly aligned and validated.

entries.pharmacophore_source: protein-peptide or peptide-like complex/pose interpreted as MOE query pharmacophore
what_it_can_teach: Article-style query points often represent ligand/peptide-side functional groups rather than receptor-complement atoms.
Strong anionic peptide-like groups should retain Neg/HBA semantics after compression.
Receptor aromatic or hydrophobic walls can justify ligand-side Aro/Hyd projection when they define shape-selective anchoring.
iteration_notes: Used in v2 smoke iteration because public structure can test chain detection, peptide interface segmentation, and MOE adapter execution.

entries.pharmacophore_source: complex/pose-derived structure-based query followed by virtual screening
what_it_can_teach: Nucleotide-like and acceptor-rich binding motifs require family dominance checks before HBA/Neg compression.
Hydrophobic/aromatic anchors should be retained only when they define independent pocket subregions.
Docking and interaction analysis are second-stage filters, not replacements for pharmacophore mechanism compression.

entries.citation_label: dual tubulin/PARP inhibitor hybrid workflow, J. Med. Chem. 2021
what_it_can_teach: Dual-target design requires preserving mechanism features that are independently meaningful for each pocket, then resolving conflicts at the molecule-design layer.
A feature that improves one target but destroys another target's essential anchor should be conditional, not automatically core.
Validation should include orthogonal assay or docking evidence because pharmacophore matching alone can overstate dual activity.

entries.pharmacophore_source: pharmacophore plus docking or downstream screening
what_it_can_teach: Hydrophobic/aromatic regions in elongated pockets should be segmented by independent anchors rather than counted as many redundant Hyd points.
Selected models should balance ligand-shape filtering with downstream docking pose plausibility.
entries.status: candidate entry; verify exact article scope before using for article-target coordinate comparison

what_it_can_teach: MD stability can promote a lower-scored pharmacophore point if it represents a persistent hotspot.
A point that vanishes across stable conformers should be auxiliary unless SAR proves it is essential.
entries.status: methodological placeholder for future confirmed literature entry

entries.citation_label: peptide-interface structure cases used for v2 rule iteration
entries.pharmacophore_source: public protein-peptide structures and article-style MOE query figures
what_it_can_teach: Backbone-register constraints should be expressed with a few HBA/HBD points, not every backbone contact.
Side-chain hotspots should be selected when they enter independent subpockets, carry dominant charge/aromatic chemistry, or lock peptide orientation.
Mechanism zones are transferable; peptide names, residue numbers, and PDB IDs are not.
entries.status: aggregate category, not a single citation

v2_rule_updates.principle: Before selection, decide whether features describe ligand/peptide-side chemistry or receptor-complement chemistry. Default to ligand-side query for MOE article-style virtual screening models.
Strong ionic groups retain Pos/Neg semantics even if they also satisfy donor/acceptor annotations.
A receptor aromatic wall can justify a ligand-side Aro feature only when it imposes shape or pi-recognition constraints; otherwise compress to Hyd or auxiliary.
Select one representative per independent mechanism zone unless two non-substitutable chemical hypotheses coexist.
MOE annotations are not final pharmacophores. They must be audited by burial, directionality, substitutability, recurrence, and validation evidence.

validation_policy: For structure-based MOE query papers: compare strict coordinate/family recovery and looser mechanism-region recovery.
For ligand-based papers: compare enrichment, GH score, active/decoy behavior, and feature-count discipline rather than PDB coordinates.
For hybrid workflows: require pharmacophore coverage plus docking/pose/SAR consistency before promoting a feature to core.
